summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones Parse Server tag 8.6.27, starts MongoDB, and verifies that an expired session token is still accepted from cache until eviction; it also verifies that extendSessionOnUse=true renews the expired session.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should show that an expired session token still authenticates a protected route until the cache entry expires, and that with extendSessionOnUse=true the expired session is renewed in storage.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the helper image, cloned Parse Server tag 8.6.27, installed dependencies, built the target, and then observed default.after_expiry_status=200, default.after_cache_ttl_status=400, default.after_cache_ttl_code=209, extend.after_expiry_status=200, extend.extended_forward=true, followed by the success marker "expired session token remained valid after DB expiry and extendSessionOnUse renewed it from the stale cache path".
verification_method: The helper creates a user, primes the session cache with /users/me, waits beyond the stored _Session.expiresAt timestamp, calls /users/me again expecting HTTP 200 before cache eviction, waits past cacheTTL expecting Parse error 209, then reruns with extendSessionOnUse=true and verifies that _Session.expiresAt moves forward.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run then printed default.after_cache_ttl_status=400 and default.after_cache_ttl_code=209, showing the token stopped working only after the cache entry aged out.

The extendSessionOnUse scenario printed extend.before_expires_at=2026-06-05T13:53:20.801Z, extend.after_expiry_status=200, extend.after_expires_at=2026-06-05T13:53:23.316Z, and extend.extended_forward=true.
The run ended with [evohunt] SUCCESS: expired session token remained valid after DB expiry and extendSessionOnUse renewed it from the stale cache path.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
